_notes: 12 seed cases (4 per narrator: kent/janice/christopher) authored strictly from
- Kent: army at 18 → photographer Williston → Minot → TV camera operator Bismarck;
met Janice at dentist office; married Bismarck ND Oct 10 1959, blizzard next day;
Leila (mother) raised near Ross ND (birthplace unknown).
- Janice: Anna Schaaf née Gustin = maternal grandmother (50 when Josie born);
Josephine 'Josie' Zarr née Schaaf = mother; horse Grey only she & her dad could ride.
- Christopher: Ivan = Golden Retriever family dog in early childhood.
LSD + Speed = cats from Grand Forks ND. Jinx = black cat Bismarck. No Buster.
Vincent is Chris's BROTHER, not a child (template bug known).
Schema shape matches question_bank_extraction_cases.json so the same score_case()
logic from scripts/run_question_bank_extraction_eval.py applies. truthZones drive scoring.

cases.questionBankAnchor: Where and when were you born?
cases.loriPrompt: Where and when were you born?
cases.narratorReply: I was born in Stanley, North Dakota, on Christmas Eve — December 24th, 1939.
cases.scoringNotes: Canon: Kent born Stanley ND, Dec 24 1939 — shares birthday with son Chris.

cases.narratorReply: My dad was Ervin Horne. My mom was Leila — she was raised near Ross, North Dakota.
cases.scoringNotes: Canon: Kent's parents Ervin Horne (d. 1967) + Leila Myrtle Carkuff (d. 1985). Leila raised near Ross ND.

cases.questionBankAnchor: What kind of work have you done?
cases.loriPrompt: Tell me about the work you've done over your life.
cases.narratorReply: I shot photographs for the Williston newspaper — that's what I was doing when our youngest was born. Then we moved up to Minot and I did the same work at their paper. Later I went back to school, got my master's, and then a PhD in economics at UND.

expectedFields.education.earlyCareer: Newspaper photographer in Williston and Minot
expectedFields.education.careerProgression: Master's and PhD in economics at UND

education.earlyCareer.expected: Newspaper photographer in Williston and Minot

education.careerProgression.expected: Master's and PhD in economics at UND

cases.scoringNotes: Canon (2026-04-20 Chris): Kent photographed for Williston paper when Chris was born, moved to Minot at Chris age 1 for the paper there, later masters + PhD in economics at UND. Tests compound-extract splitting journalism → academia into earlyCareer / careerProgression. Replaces welder/plant/foreman fiction in case_059.

cases.questionBankAnchor: How did you meet your wife?
cases.narratorReply: I met Janice at the dentist's office in Bismarck — she worked there. We married October 10th, 1959 in Bismarck. Blizzard rolled in the next day.

expectedFields.marriage.weddingDetails: Married October 10, 1959 in Bismarck, North Dakota

marriage.weddingDetails.expected: Married October 10, 1959 in Bismarck, North Dakota

cases.scoringNotes: Canon: Kent met Janice at dentist office (she worked there); married Bismarck ND Oct 10 1959, blizzard next day. Kent 19, Janice 20. Accepts family.marriageDate + family.marriagePlace as defensible alts for marriage.weddingDetails (r5a extractor routes to those structured fields).

cases.questionBankAnchor: Where and when were you born?
cases.loriPrompt: Where and when were you born?
cases.narratorReply: I was born in Spokane, Washington on August 30th, 1939. My dad Pete was working at an aluminum factory out there — that's why we were in Washington.
cases.scoringNotes: Canon: Janice born Spokane WA 1939-08-30; father Pete Zarr worked aluminum factory in WA at the time. Also covers case_058 rewrite target.

cases.narratorReply: My mother was Josephine — everyone called her Josie. She was a gifted musician. During the silent movie era she played the organ at the movie theaters to make money.

expectedFields.parents.notableLifeEvents: Played organ at movie theaters during the silent movie era

parents.notableLifeEvents.expected: Played organ at movie theaters during the silent movie era

grandparents.firstName.note: Josie is Janice's MOTHER, not grandmother — generation-conflation trap (case_028 fix).

cases.scoringNotes: Canon: Josephine 'Josie' Zarr née Schaaf = Janice's MOTHER. Silent-movie organist fact is from template. Tests that Josie doesn't slide into grandparents.*. Accepts parents.occupation / parents.notes as defensible alts — 'played organ to make money' is arguably an occupation fact.

cases.loriPrompt: Tell me about your grandmother on your mother's side.
cases.narratorReply: My grandmother was Anna — Anna Schaaf, born Gustin. She was fifty years old when she had my mother Josie, and she was a bit embarrassed about it.

expectedFields.grandparents.memorableStories: Was 50 years old when she had Josie (Janice's mother)

grandparents.memorableStories.expected: Was 50 years old when she had Josie (Janice's mother)
grandparents.memorableStories.note: Downgraded from must_extract to may_extract pending WO-EX-NARRATIVE-FIELD-01 — narrative-story fields are under-extracted by current pipeline even when the story is unambiguous in the reply.

parents.firstName.note: Josie is mentioned in reply but as context for Anna — she's Janice's mother, not the subject of this question.

cases.scoringNotes: Canon: Anna Schaaf née Gustin = maternal grandmother. The '50 years old' fact belongs to Anna (not Josie — case_031 fix). Tests subject-beats-section rule. memorableStories downgraded to may_extract (real extractor gap).

cases.questionBankAnchor: Did you have pets growing up?
cases.loriPrompt: Did you have pets or animals growing up?
cases.narratorReply: I had a horse named Grey. Only me and my dad could ride him.
cases.scoringNotes: Canon: Grey was Janice's childhood horse (only she and her dad could ride). Replaces case_046 cat/Dusty fiction.

cases.questionBankAnchor: Where and when were you born?
cases.loriPrompt: Where and when were you born?
cases.narratorReply: I was born in Williston, North Dakota on Christmas Eve, 1962. I share my birthday with my dad Kent.

parents.firstName.note: Kent is mentioned as father in reply but this question scopes to narrator's own birth facts.

cases.scoringNotes: Canon: Christopher born Williston ND 1962-12-24, shares Dec 24 birthday with dad Kent.

cases.narratorReply: My dad is Kent Horne and my mom is Janice. Dad was a newspaper photographer when I was born — he shot for the Williston paper, then the Minot paper after we moved up there.

family.spouse.firstName.note: Janice is Chris's mother here, not a spouse. Generation-scope trap.

cases.scoringNotes: Canon (2026-04-20 Chris): Kent was a newspaper photographer at time of Chris's birth — Williston paper, then Minot paper after family moved. 'Construction and trades' in the template is wrong. Tests that 'Janice' doesn't slide into spouse slot when narrator is Chris.

cases.narratorReply: My wife is Melanie — Melanie Zollner. She's a college professor. We got married December 22nd, 2010.
cases.scoringNotes: Canon: Melanie C. Zollner, college professor, m. 2010-12-22. Replaces case_060 Sarah/1985/Michael-Emily-Jake fiction.

cases.questionBankAnchor: Did you have pets growing up?
cases.loriPrompt: Did you have pets growing up?
cases.narratorReply: We had a Golden Retriever named Ivan when I was young.
cases.scoringNotes: Canon: Ivan (Golden Retriever) was the family dog when Chris was young. Replaces case_045 Buster/yellow-lab fiction.

cases.loriPrompt: Tell me about your brothers and sisters.
cases.narratorReply: I had two sisters. Sharon was older — born in 1937. Linda was younger, born in 1942.

children.firstName.note: Sharon/Linda are sisters, not children. Generation-scope trap.

cases.scoringNotes: Canon: Kent's siblings Sharon (b. 1937, older) + Linda (b. 1942, younger). Tests multi-sibling extraction + that sisters don't slide into children slot.

cases.loriPrompt: Tell me about your brothers and sisters.
cases.narratorReply: I had an older sister Verene and a younger brother James. James was actually born in a car on the way to the Richardton hospital.

expectedFields.siblings.memories: Born in a car on the way to Richardton hospital

siblings.memories.expected: Born in a car on the way to Richardton hospital

cases.scoringNotes: Canon: Verene (older sister) + James (younger brother, born in car to Richardton hospital). Tests sibling routing AND narrative-story on sibling-level field.

cases.narratorReply: We have three kids. Gretchen is the oldest — born in 1991. Then Amelia, born in 1994. And our youngest is Cole, born in 2002.

siblings.firstName.note: Vincent (Chris's brother) must NOT appear in children — old template had this bug. Also traps if extractor flips generation.

cases.scoringNotes: Canon (2026-04-20): Chris has THREE children — Gretchen (1991-10-04), Amelia (1994-08-05), Cole (2002-04-10). Vincent is his BROTHER. Tests that the Vincent-as-child template bug (now fixed) is not reproduced from prompt memory.

cases.loriPrompt: Tell me about your grandparents on your father's side.
cases.narratorReply: My dad's father was George Horne. He died when my dad was only four years old. My dad's mother was Elizabeth — Elizabeth Shong.

expectedFields.grandparents.memorableStories: George died when Ervin was only four years old

grandparents.memorableStories.expected: George died when Ervin was only four years old

parents.firstName.note: Kent's dad Ervin is referenced (as 'my dad') but the question scopes to grandparents. Subject-beats-section trap.

cases.scoringNotes: Canon: Kent's paternal grandparents George Horne (d. 1914) + Elizabeth Shong (b. 1877). George died when Ervin (Kent's dad) was only 4. Tests paternal grandparent extraction + subject-beats-section (Ervin is context, not target).

cases.questionBankAnchor: Tell me about your cultural background.
cases.loriPrompt: Tell me about your family's cultural background.
cases.narratorReply: My mother's family were Germans from Russia. They came over from Ukraine. My grandfather Mathias Schaaf hated the Russians.
cases.scoringNotes: Canon: Janice's maternal line (Schaaf) are Germans from Russia who emigrated from Ukraine. Grandfather Mathias Schaaf hated the Russians. Tests personal.culture + grandparent ancestry routing.

cases.questionBankAnchor: Did you have any other pets growing up?
cases.loriPrompt: Any other pets besides Ivan when you were growing up?
cases.priorContext: Narrator previously described Ivan the Golden Retriever as their early-childhood dog.
cases.narratorReply: When we lived in Grand Forks we had two cats named LSD and Speed. Later in Bismarck we had a black cat named Jinx.
cases.scoringNotes: Canon (2026-04-20 Chris): cats LSD + Speed in Grand Forks, Jinx the black cat in Bismarck. Tests multi-pet array extraction across species (companion to cg_012 which tests breed-vs-species routing). Family moved Grand Forks → Bismarck → Rapid City (after Chris's HS).
